clinical_study.brief_title: R-CHOP Plus Venetoclax in Richter Syndrome and CLL-Transformed DLBCL (RICHTER-VEN)
clinical_study.official_title: A Phase 2 Randomized Trial of R-CHOP (Rituximab, Cyclophosphamide, Doxorubicin, Vincristine, Prednisone) With or Without Venetoclax in Patients With Richter Syndrome or Transformation of Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia to Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma

brief_summary.textblock: This trial tests whether adding venetoclax (BCL-2 inhibitor) to standard R-CHOP chemoimmunotherapy improves complete response rate and progression-free survival in patients with Richter syndrome (RS) or transformation of chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) to diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), a condition associated with BCL2 overexpression and historically poor outcomes with standard chemoimmunotherapy.

detailed_description.textblock: Richter transformation (RT) from CLL to DLBCL occurs in approximately 2-10% of CLL patients and carries a median overall survival of 6-12 months with standard R-CHOP therapy. The transformed DLBCL commonly shows BCL2 overexpression due to t(14;18) translocation or gene amplification, MYC rearrangements (creating a dual-expressor or double-hit phenotype), and upregulation of the CLL-associated BCL2 pathway. Venetoclax, a selective BCL-2 inhibitor with established activity in CLL, may synergize with R-CHOP by overcoming BCL2-mediated chemoresistance. This study randomizes patients with biopsy-confirmed RS/CLL-DLBCL transformation to R-CHOP x 6 cycles + venetoclax 400 mg/day (starting cycle 1, day 8) versus R-CHOP x 6 cycles alone. Patients may have received prior CLL-directed therapy (BTK inhibitors, BCL-2 inhibitors, chemoimmunotherapy) provided treatment was not for RS. Stratification: prior CLL therapy (yes vs no), Ann Arbor stage (I-II vs III-IV), IPI score (0-2 vs 3-5), and MYC IHC positivity (>= 40% vs < 40%).

primary_outcome.measure: Complete Response Rate (CRR) by Lugano 2014 criteria (PET-CT) after 6 cycles
primary_outcome.time_frame: Assessed approximately 3 weeks after cycle 6 (approximately 18-19 weeks from treatment initiation)

secondary_outcome.time_frame: From randomization until radiographic progression or death, up to 24 months
From randomization until death from any cause, up to 36 months

secondary_outcome.measure: Overall Response Rate (ORR) including complete and partial responses
secondary_outcome.time_frame: After cycle 3 (interim) and after cycle 6 (end of treatment), up to 19 weeks

secondary_outcome.measure: Rate of bridging to autologous or allogeneic stem cell transplant
Safety and tolerability (NCI CTCAE v5.0), with focus on tumor lysis syndrome and myelosuppression

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria: - Documented prior diagnosis of chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) or small lymphocytic lymphoma (SLL) meeting iwCLL 2018 diagnostic criteria - Biopsy-confirmed transformation to diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL): new or growing lymph node or extranodal site with histopathologic diagnosis of DLBCL (WHO 2022 classification); DLBCL must be CD20-positive by IHC - Richter syndrome definition: DLBCL arising in the setting of CLL/SLL, with clonal relationship to the CLL clone recommended but not mandated (clonal analysis by IGH sequencing encouraged) - Dual expressor DLBCL (BCL2 IHC >= 50% and MYC IHC >= 40%) acceptable but not required; BCL2 IHC result and MYC IHC result collected at enrollment - Ann Arbor stage I-IV; any stage eligible - At least one measurable site of DLBCL disease (>= 1.5 cm in longest diameter on CT or FDG-avid on PET) - No prior systemic therapy for Richter syndrome/DLBCL transformation; prior CLL-directed therapy is allowed (BTKi, venetoclax, chemoimmunotherapy) if last dose was >= 2 weeks prior to enrollment and patient has recovered from prior toxicity - Age >= 18 years - ECOG Performance Status 0-2 - Adequate bone marrow: ANC >= 1000/mm3, platelets >= 75,000/mm3 (may be disease-related; lower thresholds permitted with documented marrow involvement by DLBCL and at investigator discretion), hemoglobin >= 8 g/dL - Adequate cardiac function: LVEF >= 50% by echocardiogram or MUGA scan (required due to doxorubicin) - Adequate hepatic function: total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN (except Gilbert syndrome), AST/ALT <= 2.5x ULN - Adequate renal function: creatinine clearance >= 40 mL/min (Cockcroft-Gault) - Signed informed consent
Exclusion Criteria: - Burkitt lymphoma, lymphoblastic lymphoma, or other transformation histologies (mantle cell, follicular) — only DLBCL transformation is eligible - EBV-associated RS (DLBCL arising in EBV-positive setting): exploratory stratum; not excluded but analyzed separately - Prior systemic therapy for Richter transformation or DLBCL (prior CLL treatment is allowed; see inclusion criteria) - Prior autologous or allogeneic stem cell transplantation within 6 months; or ongoing graft-versus-host disease requiring systemic immunosuppression - Active CNS involvement by lymphoma at enrollment (CNS prophylaxis per institutional guidelines is required and allowed) - Known HIV infection with CD4 count < 100 cells/mm3 or detectable HIV viral load; well-controlled HIV (CD4 >= 100 and undetectable viral load on antiretroviral therapy) is allowed - Active hepatitis B (HBsAg positive) without antiviral prophylaxis; HBsAg-positive patients may be enrolled with concurrent entecavir/tenofovir prophylaxis - Active hepatitis C with detectable HCV RNA - Cardiovascular contraindication to anthracycline chemotherapy (LVEF < 50%, symptomatic heart failure NYHA class III-IV, recent MI within 6 months) - History of tumor lysis syndrome with prior therapy and contraindication to venetoclax ramp-up - Concurrent strong CYP3A4 inhibitors that cannot be held during venetoclax initiation (e.g., voriconazole, posaconazole); azole antifungals must be managed per venetoclax ramp-up guidelines - Known hypersensitivity to rituximab, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, vincristine, prednisone, or venetoclax - Pregnancy or breastfeeding - Active second malignancy requiring systemic treatment

intervention.description: R-CHOP every 21 days x 6 cycles: rituximab 375 mg/m2 IV day 1, cyclophosphamide 750 mg/m2 IV day 1, doxorubicin 50 mg/m2 IV day 1, vincristine 1.4 mg/m2 IV day 1 (cap 2 mg), prednisone 100 mg orally days 1-5. Plus venetoclax ramp-up starting cycle 1 day 8: 20 mg day 8, 50 mg day 9, 100 mg day 10, 200 mg day 11, 400 mg day 12 onward (ramp-up per venetoclax TLS risk guidelines), then 400 mg/day continuously from cycle 2 through end of cycle 6.

R-CHOP every 21 days x 6 cycles: rituximab 375 mg/m2 IV day 1, cyclophosphamide 750 mg/m2 IV day 1, doxorubicin 50 mg/m2 IV day 1, vincristine 1.4 mg/m2 IV day 1 (cap 2 mg), prednisone 100 mg orally days 1-5.
